title:
How Khmer Kings' Devotion to Vishnu Shaped Angkor Wat’s Cosmic Symboli
– HD Asian Art
highlights:
Angkor Wat stands as one of the greatest architectural and spiritual masterpieces of Southeast Asia, deeply shaped by the Khmer kings’ devotion to Vishnu. Built primarily in the early 12th century under King Suryavarman II, Angkor Wat was conceived not only as a majestic temple but as a cosmic symbol and earthly abode for the great Hindu god Vishnu, reflecting the king’s religious beliefs  ...    ## Iconography and Sculptural Narratives The temple is rich with sculptural depictions of Vishnu in various forms and his legendary exploits, reinforcing the god’s dominance and the king’s affiliation with him. Bas-reliefs illustrate Vishnu’s avatars and mythological battles, underscoring themes of protection, cosmic order, and divine justice. These artistic narratives intertwine Vishnu’s  ...    prominence in the region. Buddhist statues and iconography gradually accompanied or replaced some Hindu symbols while  ...    Angkor Wat
